verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "Even though you do very virtuous actions, and perform miraculous works, yet on account of your wickedness the Lord does not know you; even so, according to the words of the Lord Himself, ‘Many will say to me in that day, Lord, Lord, have we not prophesied in Thy name? and in Thy name have cast out devils? and in Thy name done many wonderful works?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: And then will I profess unto them, I never knew you; depart from me, ye that work iniquity.’"

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: We acknowledge the word of the Lord.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Hence also the apostle says, "Though I have all faith, so that I could remove mountains, and have not charity, I am nothing."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Here therefore we must inquire who it is that has charity: you will find that it is no one else but those who are lovers of unity.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: For as to the driving out of devils, and as to the working of miracles, seeing that very many do not do such things who yet belong to the kingdom of God, and very many do them who do not belong to it, neither our party nor your party have any cause for boasting, if any of them chance to have this power, since the Lord did not think it right that even the apostles, who could truly do such things both to profit and salvation, should boast in things like this, when He says to them, "In this rejoice not, that the spirits are subject unto you; but rather rejoice, because your names are written in heaven."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Wherefore all those things which you have advanced from the writings of the gospel I also might repeat to you, if I saw you working the powerful acts of signs and miracles; and so might you repeat them to me, if you saw me doing things of a like sort.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Let us not, therefore, say one to another what may equally be said on the other side as well; and, putting aside all quibbles, since we are inquiring where the Church of Christ is to be found, let us listen to the words of Christ Himself, who redeemed it with His own blood: "Ye shall be witnesses unto me both in Jerusalem, and in all Judea, and in Samaria, and even in the whole earth."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: You see then who it is with whom a man refuses to communicate who will not communicate with this Church, which is spread throughout all the world, if at least you hear whose words these are.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: For what is a greater proof of madness than to hold communion with the sacraments of the Lord, and to refuse to hold communion with the words of the Lord?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Such men at any rate are likely to say, In Thy name have we eaten and drunken, and to hear the words, "I never knew you," seeing that they eat His body and drink His blood in the sacrament, and do not recognize in the gospel His members which are spread abroad throughout the earth, and therefore are not themselves counted among them in the judgment.
